tagline: Hike smarter. Stay safer. Share more.
elevator_pitch: WeTrail is a hiking companion app that lets you download offline trail maps to your smartwatch, check in with loved ones via GPS breadcrumbs, and share your adventures through a social network built for hikers. It combines the safety of a personal locator with the community of Strava, purpose-built for the trail.
problem: Hikers venturing into national parks and backcountry areas lose cell service almost immediately, leaving them without navigation tools or any way to communicate their location. Every year, hundreds of hikers get lost or injured on trails with no way to signal for help. Existing trail info is scattered across clunky government websites, making it unnecessarily hard to plan hikes and assess real-time conditions.
solution: WeTrail lets hikers download detailed trail maps to their phone and Apple Watch before heading out, so they always have navigation even without cell service. A built-in check-in system logs your planned trail, pings GPS breadcrumbs along the way, and alerts your safety contacts if something seems off. A social feed lets hikers share photos, trail conditions, wildlife sightings, and difficulty ratings pinned to exact map locations.
target_market: Outdoor enthusiasts aged 25-45 who hike regularly in national parks, state parks, and backcountry areas. Apple Watch and smartwatch owners who want wrist-based navigation. Safety-conscious hikers, solo female hikers, and families who want peace of mind on the trail.
revenue_model: Freemium model: free tier includes top 10 trails per major national park with basic offline maps. Premium subscription at $4.99/month unlocks all trails (state parks, national forests, backcountry routes), advanced safety features, weather-synced trail alerts, and detailed social analytics. Additional revenue from targeted ads for hiking gear brands and affiliate partnerships with outdoor retailers.
key_features: Offline trail maps downloaded directly to your smartwatch for navigation without cell service
Safety check-in system with GPS breadcrumb tracking and emergency contact alerts
Social trail feed with geo-tagged photos, wildlife sightings, and real-time condition reports
Smart trail recommendations based on skill level, weather, and crowd density
competitive_advantage: WeTrail is the only app that combines offline smartwatch maps, active safety monitoring, and a hiker-specific social network in one platform. While AllTrails offers trail info and Strava tracks fitness, neither provides offline watch navigation paired with real-time safety check-ins. The social layer creates a network effect where each new user makes trail data more accurate and valuable for everyone.
market_size_estimate: The US outdoor recreation economy generates over $862 billion annually. Over 60 million Americans go hiking each year. The hiking app market (AllTrails, Gaia GPS) is estimated at $200M+. With a target of capturing 2-3% of active hikers at $4.99/month premium, the addressable market is roughly $180M-$270M annually.
go_to_market: Launch with the 10 most popular national parks (Glacier, Yellowstone, Zion, Yosemite, etc.) as a free beta to build initial user base and trail data. Partner with park visitor centers and outdoor gear shops for co-marketing. Seed the social network by recruiting hiking influencers and trail running communities to share content early.
risks: Competing against well-established apps like AllTrails and Gaia GPS that already have large user bases and trail databases
GPS accuracy limitations on smartwatches in deep canyons and dense forest cover could undermine the core safety promise
Building a social network requires critical mass of users before it becomes valuable, creating a chicken-and-egg adoption challenge
year_one_milestones: Launch MVP with offline maps and safety check-ins for 10 major national parks, targeting 50,000 downloads
Build active social community of 10,000 monthly contributors sharing trail photos and condition reports
Secure 3 outdoor brand partnerships (e.g., REI, Merrell, Osprey) for in-app advertising and co-marketing
